name: Pear Souffle with Raspberry Coulis
recipeIngredient: 2 cans pear halves in heavy syrup, drained (15 oz.)
1/4 cup poire william or other pear brandy or spirit of your choice, optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease the bottom and sides of a 2-quart (2 L) souffle dish with butter or margarine.
2. Sprinkle 3 tablespoons (30 ml) of sugar evenly inside the dish. Pour out any excess.
3. Cut 2 pear halves into 1/4 inch (5 mm) dice and set aside.
4. Add the remaining pears to an electric blender or food processor, along with 1/3 cup (80 ml) sugar. Puree until smooth.
5. Pour the pear puree into a saucepan and cook over medium heat for 20 to 30 minutes, until thickened and reduced to about 1½ cups (375 ml).
6. In a small bowl, dissolve cornstarch in lemon juice and add it to the cooked pears. Continue cooking until the mixture boils and thickens, about 3 minutes.
7. Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature. Stir in the optional pear liqueur.
8. In a large, clean bowl, beat egg whites and salt until foamy. Add the remaining 3 tablespoons (15 ml) sugar and continue beating until stiff, glossy peaks form.
9. Mix 1/4 of the egg whites with the cooked pear base to lighten it, then gently fold in the remaining whites just until blended – do not over mix.
10. Spoon half the souffle mixture into the prepared souffle dish and sprinkle with half the diced pears. Add the remaining souffle mixture and top with the remaining diced pears.
11. Bake in a 425°F (220°C) oven for about 15 minutes, until well puffed and golden in color. Serve immediately, topped with raspberry coulis. Serves 6 to 8.
